title: Athyrium plantaginifolium
taxonomy: Athyrium plantaginifolium is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically belonging to the division Pteridophyta. It falls under the class Polypodiopsida and is a part of the family Athyriaceae. The genus Athyrium is a group of ferns, with A. plantaginifolium being one of the species. The classification is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, division - Pteridophyta, class - Polypodiopsida, family - Athyriaceae, genus - Athyrium, species - A. plantaginifolium. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
uses: Athyrium plantaginifolium, a species of fern native to China, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are said to be rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Athyrium plantaginifolium is believed to aid in digestion, relieve fever, and reduce swelling. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Athyrium plantaginifolium for medicinal purposes. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.
benefits: Athyrium plantaginifolium, also known as the alpine lady fern, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties.
